name: The Backstage Handbook: An Illustrated Almanac of Technical Information
desc: This handy reference book brings together an incredible variety of information useful to designers, technicians, and students in theatre, film, and television. Includes chapters on tools, hardware, materials, electrics, shop math, and architecture with hundreds of illustrations, tables, and charts.

name: CTS Certified Technology Specialist Exam Guide, Third Edition
desc: Published with InfoComm International, CTS Certified Technology Specialist Exam Guide, Second Edition provides comprehensive coverage of all objectives on the latest release of the InfoComm Certified Technology Specialist exam―the leading internationally recognized audiovisual (AV) certification. You’ll get learning objectives at the beginning of each chapter, best practices, checklists, diagrams, photos, chapter review questions with in-depth explanations, and a full-color insert. Designed to help you prepare for the CTS exam, this authoritative resource also serves as an essential on-the-job reference.

name: Stagehand 101
desc: This book is a comprehensive manual to help you become a knowledgeable and in demand Stagehand for Live Concerts and Event Production.

name: Technical Theater for Nontechnical People
desc: In this thoroughly revised new edition, the popular guide firmly embraces the digital age with new content about digital audio, intelligent lighting, LED lighting, video projection, and show control systems, all explained in the same approachable style that has kept this book in the pockets of industry professionals for many years. A brand-new chapter on sound design has also been added, and every chapter has been updated with more information about the basics of theater technology, including draperies, lighting instruments, microphones, costume sketches, and more.

name: The Sound Reinforcement Handbook
desc: (Yamaha Products). Sound reinforcement is the use of audio amplification systems. This book is the first and only book of its kind to cover all aspects of designing and using such systems for public address and musical performance. The book features information on both the audio theory involved and the practical applications of that theory, explaining everything from microphones to loudspeakers. This revised edition features almost 40 new pages and is even easier to follow with the addition of an index and a simplified page and chapter numbering system. New topics covered include: MIDI, Synchronization, and an Appendix on Logarithms. 416 Pages.

name: Career Projection 101: An Independent Contractor's Guide to a Successful Business and Balanced Life
desc: Professional Projectionist, Clem Harrod, brings over 25 years of knowledge and experience to his company CLEMCO.AV. He established the organization with the mission to Coach, Lead, Educate and Mentor those studying Audio-Visual Technology and working in the Live Event Production Industry. Clem now focuses his energy on helping Independent Contractors enjoy a life with less stress and more financial opportunities. Through his career in Video Projection, Clem began to understand Life Projection in ways that allowed him to amplify his success.

name: The Ultimate Live Sound Operator's Handbook
desc: The second edition of The Ultimate Live Sound Operator's Handbook is updated with new text, photos, illustrations, and video examples to specifically address the important, cutting-edge concerns faced by modern sound operators of all types. High-quality audio is increasingly imperative, whether you're running sound for a rock, country, punk, or jazz band performing in clubs, arenas, or outdoor parks. With the advent and implementation of large-budget multimedia presentations, along with high-resolution multichannel audio for movies, television, and downloads, any live act must sound great to be well received by today's increasingly savvy audience members. This revised handbook focuses on each aspect of live sound in a way that is straightforward and easy to understand, breaking the process down into principles and practices that assist the modern sound tech in everything from planning and budgeting to mixing and recording the live show.

name: Bob McCarthy: Sound System Design and Optimization
desc: The definitive professional reference for sound system design and optimization. Covers speaker array theory, system alignment, delay, equalization, and measurement methodology used by top touring and install audio engineers worldwide. Considered essential reading for any serious live sound or AV systems professional.

name: Yamaha Sound Reinforcement Handbook
desc: Published by Yamaha, this comprehensive handbook covers the theory and practice of sound reinforcement — from acoustics and signal flow to loudspeakers, amplifiers, and system design. A widely referenced standard reference for audio engineers and system designers in live sound.

name: Introduction to Show Control
desc: The foundational reference for show control systems in themed entertainment, concerts, and live events. Covers timecode, MIDI, OSC, serial communication, and integration strategies for complex multi-system productions. Written by one of the industry's leading show control authorities.

name: Rock Solid Ethernet
desc: Practical guide to Ethernet networking specifically written for entertainment technology professionals. Covers network fundamentals, managed switches, VLANs, and the specific requirements of Dante, sACN, and other AV-over-IP protocols. An essential reference for AV techs who work with networked audio and lighting systems.

name: Recommended Practice of DMX512
desc: The authoritative practical guide to DMX512 protocol for lighting and entertainment control professionals. Covers DMX fundamentals, cable infrastructure, termination, troubleshooting, and best practices drawn directly from the ESTA standard. A must-have reference for lighting technicians working with DMX-controlled systems.

name: Electricity for Entertainment Electricians & Technicians by Richard Cadena
desc: Comprehensive practical study guide for entertainment industry power distribution, written by ETCP Recognized Trainer Richard Cadena. Covers AC/DC fundamentals, 3-phase power, grounding, safety, generators, and transformer calculations with real-world examples. The primary resource for anyone preparing for ETCP Entertainment Electrician certification.

name: NFPA 70: National Electrical Code
desc: The National Electrical Code — the authoritative standard for electrical wiring and safety in the United States. Covers installation requirements, conductor sizing, overcurrent protection, grounding, and special occupancies including assembly venues and stages. Required knowledge for production electricians and AV integrators working on permanent installations.

name: Soares Book on Grounding and Bonding
desc: The Soares Book on Grounding and Bonding is the authoritative reference for understanding and applying the NEC grounding and bonding requirements. Essential reading for electrical professionals and production technicians responsible for power distribution and electrical safety in live event environments.

name: Overcurrents and Undercurrents by Earl W. Roberts
desc: A detailed examination of overcurrent protection, electrical safety, and NEC compliance for entertainment industry power systems. Covers breaker selection, feeder sizing, fault current analysis, and the unique challenges of portable power distribution in live event environments. Written specifically for entertainment industry electricians.

name: The MIDI Manual: A Practical Guide to MIDI in the Project Studio
desc: A comprehensive and practical introduction to MIDI — covering MIDI messages, sequencing, synchronization, and implementation for studio and live production environments. Accessible to beginners and useful as a reference for working engineers using MIDI-controlled systems.

name: Augmented Reality in Public Spaces: Basic Techniques for Video Mapping Vol. 1
desc: This book, the first in a series, dedicated to New Technologies for the Arts, is aimed at all digital arts students (PC and MAC users) who want to try video mapping for the first time. Thanks to a course of reading that alternates theoretic concepts and practical exercises - explaining from how to take a photograph to how to request permission, from the layer masks to 2D and 3D animation, up to the final projection and warping - the reader will be able to project a video mapping event.

name: How Video Works: From Broadcast to the Cloud
desc: A thorough technical guide covering how video signals are created, processed, distributed, and displayed — from broadcast SD and HD through IP video and cloud delivery. Essential foundational knowledge for video engineers and AV professionals working across broadcast and live event production.

name: Media Design and Technology for Live Events
desc: Media Design and Technology for Live Entertainment is a guide to understanding the concepts and equipment used in projection and video design for live performances. After an introduction in the principles of design elements as well as information on content, this book focuses on how content is used and transmitted by describing the essential components of systems, providing definitions used in communicating video concepts, and including basic system troubleshooting tips and tricks. A brief history of projected imagery is included, as well as information on analog systems, as outdated technology continues to be used either by choice of the designer or by necessity due to budget. By providing the information to understand the tools and how to use them, the reader should be able to create their own systems to meet his or her design ideas.

name: Digital Video Pocket Guide
desc: Compact field reference covering digital video formats, signal types, codec specifications, and connectivity standards used in professional video production. Designed to be kept on hand for quick lookup of technical specifications during shoots, installations, and system commissioning. A concise reference for video engineers and AV integrators.

name: Displays: Fundamentals & Applications, Second Edition
desc:
In the extensive fields of optics, holography and virtual reality, technology continues to evolve. Displays: Fundamentals and Applications, Second Edition addresses these updates and discusses how real-time computer graphics and vision enable the application and displays of graphical 2D and 3D content.

This book explores in detail these technological developments, as well as the shifting techniques behind projection displays, projector-camera systems, stereoscopic and autostereoscopic displays. This new edition contains many updates and additions reflecting the changes in fast developing areas such as holography and near-eye displays for Augmented and Virtual reality applications.

Perfect for the student looking to sharpen their developing skill or the master refining their technique, Rolf Hainich and Oliver Bimber help the reader understand the basics of optics, light modulation, visual perception, display technologies, and computer-generated holography. With almost 500 illustrations Displays will help the reader see the field of augmentation and virtual reality display with new eyes.

name: Projection Display, Second Edition
desc: A comprehensive technical reference covering projection display technologies including DLP, LCD, laser, and CRT projectors. Covers optics, color science, screen technology, and system integration for AV professionals specifying and deploying large-screen projection systems.

name: Screen Producing & Media Operations
desc:
Media servers have established themselves as the dominant video playback tool for live events; however, the practice of delivering content to these systems and the structure of the media operations team is still evolving.

This book outlines a workflow for video content delivery and describes team communication that can be applied to any entertainment production including: television specials, concert touring, corporate events, theater, as well as special events, film, large audience marketing events, and multi-screen permanent installations. This workflow is hardware and software independent, designed to evolve with future technologies as they become established in the field of multi-screen production, and has been proven professionally by the author and her peers over a decade of productions.

The methodology presented will provide insights beneficial to students and current practitioners of media server technology, screens producers, and video content developers. Using real world examples of internationally recognized productions, a foundation is laid for best practices in Media Operations.

Additional content, including full-color versions of the images inside the book, is available online.

name: The Wide Guide: Blueprint for the Multiscreen Movement
desc: Reference guide covering multi-screen and widescreen video production techniques, content creation strategies, and technical requirements for panoramic display systems. Addresses the creative and technical challenges of producing content for wide-format and multi-screen event installations. A specialized resource for video designers and content creators working in large-format live event production.

name: Augmented Reality in Public Spaces Vol. 2
desc: This book, the first in a series, dedicated to New Technologies for the Arts, is aimed at all digital arts students (PC and MAC users) who want to try video mapping for the first time. Thanks to a course of reading that alternates theoretic concepts and practical exercises - explaining from how to take a photograph to how to request permission, from the layer masks to 2D and 3D animation, up to the final projection and warping - the reader will be able to project a video mapping event.

name: A Broadcast Engineering Tutorial for Non-Engineers, Third Edition
desc: Graham Jones has more than 35 years experience in the broadcast industry. With NAB he works on advanced television issues, technical standards, education and training. He is a member of both ATSC and SMPTE engineering committees. Previously he was Engineering Director for the Harris/PBS DTV Express - the educational road show that introduced DTV to many U.S. broadcasters. He started his career with the BBC in London, and has worked as a consultant to broadcasters in many parts of the world. He holds a degree in physics, is a chartered electrical engineer, and is a member of the IEE, SMPTE, SBE and the Royal Television Society. In 2004 he received the Bernard J. Lechner Outstanding Contributor Award from the Advanced Television Systems Committee.

name: Practical Cinematography, Second Edition
desc: Practical guide to cinematography covering camera operation, exposure, lenses, lighting for camera, and creative visual storytelling techniques. Applicable to AV professionals who work in video production, broadcast, and live event camera roles. A concise, field-ready reference written for working cinematographers rather than students.

name: FIAF Digital Projection Guide
desc: FIAF Digital Projection Guide addresses the technical challenges that cinémathèques, archival and repertory cinemas and festivals encounter in the paradigm change from analogue film projection to digital cinema. The guide is an extension of, and update to, The Advanced Projection Manual (2006), a book covering the craft of projecting film classics with modern equipment.

name: Video Production Disciplines and Techniques, 11th Edition
desc: This popular book introduces readers to the operations underlying video production. It provides thorough coverage of the theory readers need to know, balancing complexity with practical how-to information about detailed subjects, and it does so in a concise, conversational style. The authors have incorporated the major changes that have occurred in recent years; further increased the emphasis on digital, non-linear video production; updated and expanded information on mobile technologies; and added more than 25 new or updated figures. The subtitle remains disciplines and techniques because focus of the book continues to be on the fact that students need those foundations in order to be successful in video production, no matter where they may end up. Its affordable, student-friendly price, companion website, and print book and ebook options add to this book's practical nature.

name: Video Production Handbook, Fifth Edition
desc: A practical guide to video production covering cameras, lighting, audio, editing, and distribution workflows. Covers both traditional broadcast techniques and modern digital production methods, making it a useful reference for AV professionals working in video production and live broadcast environments.

name: Vision and Art: The Biology of Seeing
desc: Margaret Livingstone's exploration of the neuroscience of visual perception and how the brain processes what we see. Covers contrast, color, motion, and depth perception with implications for art, display technology, and visual media design. Fascinating reading for video engineers and lighting designers.

name: Color Science and the Visual Arts
desc:
A curator, a paintings conservator, a photographer, and a conservation scientist walk into a bar.” What happens next? In lively and accessible prose, color science expert Roy S. Berns helps the reader understand complex color-technology concepts and offers solutions to problems that occur when art is displayed, conserved, imaged, or reproduced.
 
Berns writes for two types of audiences: museum professionals seeking explanations for common color-related issues and students in conservation, museum studies, and art history programs. The seven chapters in the book fall naturally into two sections: fundamentals, covering topics such as spectral measurements, metamerism, and color inconstancy; and applications, where artwork display, painting materials, and color reproduction are discussed. A unique feature of this book is the use of more than 200 images as its main medium of communication, employing color physics, color vision, and imaging science to produce visualizations throughout the pages. An annotated bibliography complements the main text with suggestions for further reading and more in-depth study of particular topics.

name: Color & Light: Navigating Color Mixing in the Midst of an LED Revolution
desc: Color & Light is an essential practical guide to how color works in light. Written from the perspective of a theatrical lighting designer, it discusses how to see color, how to construct effective lighting palettes, and how to make use of both color filters and color-mixing LED fixtures to create compositions that work well with scenery and costumes to tell compelling stories. We are presently at the leading edge of a revolution in theatrical lighting, redefining how it can be used to create and communicate. Today s LED-based additive color-mixing fixtures require new methodologies and new ways of thinking, and Color & Light directly addresses this technology s potentials and challenges. But underpinning lighting s many recent technological changes is the fundamental language of color that artists have worked with since the birth of humanity s artistic urges. More saturated vs. less, warmer vs. cooler, more green vs. more magenta, recessive vs. dominant the bedrock language of color manipulation endures, whether you are putting paintbrush to canvas or calling up color attributes on a high-tech lighting console. Balancing the basics and the latest innovations, Color & Light is a book for everyone involved artistically with light and color. It will enhance your understanding of how a lighting setup works from a color perspective, while equipping you with the language to communicate about color with your collaborators.

name: Television Production Handbook, Eleventh Edition
desc: In the field-defining text TELEVISION PRODUCTION HANDBOOK, author Herbert Zettl emphasizes how production proceeds in the digital age-from idea to image-and how it moves through the three major phases, from preproduction to production to postproduction. In this context, readers will learn about the necessary tools, examine what they can and cannot do, and explore how they are used to ensure maximum efficiency and effectiveness. This edition features the latest digital equipment and production techniques, including including stereo 3D, 3D camcorders, 4K and 8K digital cinema cameras, portable switchers, LED lighting instruments, and digital lighting control systems.

name: Selected Tables, Charts and Formulas for the Student Cinematographer (ASC)
desc: Excerpted reference tables and formulas from the American Society of Cinematographers Manual covering exposure, depth of field, lens focal lengths, and film/sensor formats. A compact technical reference for camera operators and video engineers who need quick access to cinematography calculations. Useful for AV professionals working in broadcast and video production environments.

name: American Cinematographer Manual, 10th Edition
desc: The Highly-Anticipated 10th Edition of the American Cinematographer Manual is Now Available from the American Society of Cinematographers Known as the Filmmaker s bible, for several generations, one of the world s most widely used cinema reference books is more comprehensive than ever moving into the digital side of image capture. The editing of the 10th AC Manual was overseen by Michael Goi, ASC, former ASC President. He is a key speaker on issues involving technology and the history of cinema. Completely re-imagined to reflect the sweeping technological changes that our industry has experienced, this edition of the Manual is vibrant and essential reading, as well as an invaluable field resource,

name: Mastering Aperture, Shutter Speed, ISO and Exposure
desc: There are numerous books on this topic, so I appreciate your interest in mine.  I have made a concerted effort to write a book that is comprehensive yet easy to follow and understand.  I would appreciate any feedback that you are willing to offer.  My contact information is in all my books.

name: From Page to Stage
desc:
How does a designer harness something as elusive as the human imagination to create a set that will complement and enhance a dramatic production? What steps are involved in making the jump from a script's text to an engaging imaginative stage?
In From Page to Stage, author Rosemary Ingham explores the relationships between text analysis, imagination, and creation. Heavily illustrated with striking examples

name: Setting Up Your Shots: Great Camera Moves Every Filmmaker Should Know
desc: A visual reference guide for cinematographers and video directors covering over 100 essential camera moves and compositions with diagrams and examples. A practical reference for video directors, content creators, and live event camera operators working in broadcast and live production.

name: Set Lighting Technician's Handbook
desc: The comprehensive on-set reference for gaffers, lighting technicians, and cinematographers working in film and television production. Covers electrical safety, lighting equipment, grip hardware, generator operation, and practical lighting techniques for production environments.

name: Basics of Video Lighting
desc: An accessible introduction to the principles and practice of video lighting for broadcast, film, and corporate video production. Covers light sources, color temperature, three-point lighting, location lighting, and practical techniques for achieving professional-quality video images.

name: The Advanced Projection Manual
desc:
The Advanced Projection Manual is designed to provide cinema engineers and projectionists with the necessary technical know-how and hands-on advice. The book also includes a much needed introduction to electronic and digital projection technologies.

The Advanced Projection Manual is published jointly by FIAF and the Norwegian Film Institute.

name: A Method of Lighting the Stage
desc: Demonstrates the techniques of creating visual effects in theatrical productions through a variety of techniques

name: Lighting and the Design Idea
desc: Understand the design process with LIGHTING AND THE DESIGN IDEA, Third Edition! With coverage of professional theatre practices in both commercial and residential theater, this text emphasizes the process by which lighting designers turn their ideas about a piece into the medium of stage lighting. Design concepts are illustrated by the text's art program so that concepts are easier to apply. A new chapter provides real-world advice, sample resumes, and other helpful tools for making the transition from student to working professional.

name: Introduction to the Musical Art of Stage Lighting Design
desc: Authored by an internationally accomplished stage lighting designer and educator, this book is an introduction to the tools and techniques of stage lighting design. It is filled with production photos and illustrations of stage lighting fixtures, controllers, automated lighting fixtures, and professional design paperwork and processes. The book also covers the process of lighting design and its relationship to the compositional structure and qualities of music. Chapters on the lighting team and lighting paperwork are included. The Fourth Edition has updated text and photos on new lighting technology and a new chapter on Lighting Research. This book is an excellent resource for any beginning or intermediate course in stage lighting design.

name: The Stage Lighting Handbook
desc: The Stage Lighting Handbook is well established as the classic practical lighting guide. The book explains the process of designing lighting for all forms of stage production and describes the equipment used. This new edition includes up-to-date information on new equipment and discusses its impact on working methods.

name: Bullet Proof I Wish I Was: The Lighting & Stage Design of Andi Watson
desc:
Christopher Scoates is the director of the University Art Museum, California State University, Long Beach. He lives in Southern California.

Thom Yorke is the lead vocalist and songwriter for Radiohead.

name: The VJ Book: Inspirations and Practical Advice for Live Visual Performance
desc: Comprehensive guide to live visual performance covering VJ culture, software tools, hardware setups, content creation, and performance techniques. Written for both beginners and experienced VJs, it covers the creative and technical aspects of real-time video mixing for clubs, concerts, and events. A foundational text for anyone working in live video and media server environments.

name: Practical Home Theater: A Guide to Video and Audio Systems
desc: Comprehensive reference for designing and integrating home theater audio and video systems covering display technologies, audio formats, speaker placement, signal distribution, and system calibration. Useful background reading for AV integrators who work in the residential sector alongside commercial installations.

name: HDMI Uncensored / Inside HDMI
desc: Technical deep-dive into the HDMI specification covering signal structure, TMDS encoding, HDCP copy protection, EDID handshaking, and common implementation issues. Written for AV engineers and integrators who need to understand the protocol at a technical level for troubleshooting and system design. A practical reference for anyone dealing with HDMI signal chain problems in the field.

name: The Lighting Handbook
desc: The Bible for anyone who is serious about lighting. Covers all technologies, recommended applications and illuminance recommendations and much, much more. Brand new edition.

name: Concert Lighting: Technique, Art and Business
desc:
Concert Lighting is a comprehensive book on lighting design for concerts. Placing special emphasis on rock-and-roll concert lighting equipment and techniques, the book takes its reader on tour, covering every aspect of that experience for the touring professional lighting technician and designer. It also provides several chapters to cross-media use of concert lighting techniques. Discussions of applications in film, video, the theatre, and the corporate world demonstrate the ways in which today's lighting designers cross over into other design areas.

Covering computer-aided drafting, moving lights, hi-bred consoles, concert techniques in television production, and featuring designs by some of the top concert designers in the industry, Concert Lighting is designed to assist students and professionals in understanding the unique fixtures, structures, special effects and design elements used in concert lighting today.

* New Information on computer aided drafting and visualization programs, moving luminaires, digital luminaires and media servers, LED luminaires, new Protocol devices and more to keep you up to date with the most cutting edge trends * Everything a professional needs to know from fighting for contracts to the ins and outs of designing a show by current leading designers: Jeff Ravitz, Andi Watson, Paul Dexter, and legendary Broadway designer Richard Pilbrow * Learn how to light rock-and-roll concerts and use the same techniques in film, video, theatre other medias * Interviews with three established, multi-platinum touring recording artists

name: The Magic of Light
desc: Jean Rosenthal's landmark book on theatrical lighting design — one of the first comprehensive treatments of the craft written by a working professional designer. Covers design philosophy, color, equipment, and the creative process of theatrical lighting from one of Broadway's pioneering lighting designers.

name: Photometrics Handbook, Second Edition
desc: The Photometrics Handbook provides standardized data on stage lighting instruments including beam angles, field angles, and photometric tables for incandescent, LED, and discharge fixtures. An essential reference for lighting designers specifying fixtures and calculating coverage in theatrical and live event environments.

name: The Assistant Lighting Designer's Toolkit
desc:
What are the do’s and don’ts of being a good assistant lighting designer?

What are focus tapes, and how do I use them?

What is the best method for creating a magic sheet?

What should be found in every assistant’s kit?

How do I make that first important leap into this professional career?

Answer these questions and many more with The Assistant Lighting Designer’s Toolkit. This definitive guide unlocks the insider-secrets used to succeed as a professional assistant lighting designer (ALD) – whether choosing assisting as a career or while transitioning to another. This book outlines, step-by-step, the challenges the ALD faces during every phase of production. Never before has a resource existed that views the design process through the eyes of the assistant.

Intermingled among the nuts and bolts of the paperwork and essential procedures, top industry professionals reveal tips for personal survival in this challenging career – both domestically and abroad as well as in other careers in lighting. Within these pages are the industry secrets rarely taught in school!

name: The Automated Lighting Programmer's Handbook
desc: This guide helps lighting designers with the creative and operational challenges they face in their rapidly evolving industry. Providing respected and clear coverage of the process of programming automated lighting fixtures, the author brings the designer from basic principles to preproduction preparations. Concepts, procedures, and guidelines to ensure a successful production are covered as well as troubleshooting, much needed information on work relationships, and technology including LED lighting, console networking, digital lighting, and more. Chapters are peppered with advice and war stories from some of the most prominent lighting designers of today.

name: The Unplugged Guitarist
desc: A guide to playing acoustic guitar without amplification — covering technique, tone production, and the unique challenges of performing unamplified in various acoustic environments. Relevant for sound engineers who work with acoustic instruments and understand the relationship between instrument, player, and room.

name: Theater Technology
desc:
The most recent innovation brought to theater technology by the eminent theater designer George C. Izenour is a "trans-sondant" curtain, mimicking a wall, which allows halls visually to metamorphose into small or large performance spaces while retaining optimum acoustic quality in both guises. A full set of drawings and photographs of the first trans-sondant structure, in the Vern Riffe Center for the Arts (1995), is one of the new features in the second edition of this incomparable reference guide to theater engineering and technology. 

Izenour ties detailed information on construction, lighting, acoustical structures, electro-mechanical-hydraulic systems, and stage controls to a rich history of technological developments from the invention of the proscenium stage in late Renaissance Italy to the contributions of our own time. All the drawings are produced on the same scale for plan, transverse section, and perspective section. Theater Technology is a reference and source of ideas and inspiration for students, teachers, and practitioners in fields including acoustics, structural engineering, design, lighting, and architecture for the theater. For theater and architecture buffs it offers a detailed and illustrated history of the field and speculation on its future directions.

name: Theatre Backstage from A to Z
desc:
This invaluable guide to theatrical production includes up-to-date definitions of all technical aspects of stagecraft, including the many advances in the past decade, particularly in the areas of trusses, rigging, and automated lights and their control. More than 200 terms, along with 25 new drawings, have been added, many existing definitions have been expanded and drawings revised to include new technological developments, and World Wide Web addresses have been added to the List of Manufacturers and Distributors and to the Bibliography. At the same time, older terminology and definitions have been retained so the book will remain useful for those unable to participate in the high-tech revolution.

For more than thirty years this manual has been the essential handbook for both the beginner and the advanced backstage technician. At a time when theatre, both professional and amateur, is growing and flourishing all over the United States, Theatre Backstage from A to Z provides more practical information, on a wider range of subjects, than can be found in any other book in the field.

name: Vectorworks for Entertainment Design
desc: A comprehensive guide to using Vectorworks Spotlight software for entertainment production design — covering lighting plots, rigging plans, paperwork management, and 3D visualization. Essential for lighting designers and production designers using Vectorworks as their primary design and documentation tool.

name: Stage Lighting Design
desc: Stage Lighting Design is a comprehensive introduction to technical theatre, tracing the evolution of lighting design from ancient drama to contemporary performance. Neil Fraser covers everything that today's designers will need to know, from the simple nuts and bolts of equipment, through to the complexity of a full lighting rig, including all aspects of the stage electrician and lighter designer's roles. This revised second edition includes new material on historical development, intelligent control systems and the latest advances in LED fixtures and luminaires. Each chapter includes key exercises, now totalling 100, that enable the reader to practise their skills on a wide variety of lighting challenges. The work of current designers is showcased and analysed, with examples from complete and detailed lighting designs.Includes: Choosing and using equipment; Applying colour; Techniques for focusing; Lighting in the round and other stage layouts; Creating mood and atmosphere; Lighting effects and LED source fixtures; Planning, testing and executing a lighting design.Superbly llustrated with over 150 colour, black & white photographs and line artworks.

name: Stage Lighting
desc: Stage Lighting: The Fundamentals is written specifically for introductory stage lighting courses. The book begins with an examination of the nature of light, perception, and color, then leads into a conversation of stage lighting equipment and technicians. Lamps, luminaries, controls/dimming, and electricity form the basis of these chapters. The book also provides a detailed explanation and overview of the lighting design process for the theatre and several other traditional forms of entertainment. Finally, the book explores a variety of additional areas where lighting designers can find related future employment, such as concert and corporate lighting, themed design, architectural and landscape lighting, and computer animation.

name: The Practical Guide to Stage Lighting
desc:
In the first edition of A Practical Guide to Stage Lighting, Steve Shelley cracked open his production book and showed how to prepare a lighting design and create the paperwork needed to mount a production.

In the second edition, he pulled back the curtain and showed the methods and processes that go on before the light plot is finalized and ready to go to into the shop, even dealing with cutting the plot in half.

In this third edition, Shelley throws the door wide open and shows step-by-step how to construct every lighting system in the Hokey light plot. Combining his diacritical analysis, killer drafting, and analytic use of the Slinky Method and Slinky Calculations, he presents the Periodic Table of Fundamental Lighting Systems and shows the basic methods used to create multi-instrument lighting systems.

name: Rocket Reference: Audio/Video Cable Installer's Pocket Guide
desc: Pocket-sized field reference for AV cable installers covering connector pinouts, cable specifications, signal levels, and wiring standards for audio and video systems. Designed to be carried on the job for quick reference during cable fabrication and system installation. A practical tool for AV technicians who do hands-on cabling work.

name: Digital Video Pocket Guide (Updated Edition)
desc: Compact field reference covering digital video formats, signal types, codec specifications, and connectivity standards used in professional video production. Designed to be kept on hand for quick lookup of technical specifications during shoots, installations, and system commissioning. A concise reference for video engineers and AV integrators.

name: Automated Lighting: The Art and Science of Moving and Color-Changing Lights
desc: Automated Lighting: The Art and Science of Moving and Color-Changing Lights, Third Edition (formerly Automated Lighting: The Art and Science of Moving Light) continues to be the most trusted text for working and aspiring lighting professionals. Now in its third edition, it has been fully updated to reflect the vast changes in stage and studio luminairies―including LEDs, switch-mode power supplies, optics, networking, Ethernet-based protocols like Art-Net and sACN, wireless DMX, and much more. Its written in clear, easy-to-understand language and includes enough detailed information to benefit for the most experienced technicians, programmers, and designers. Additional content and resources are provided at the author's website www.automatedlighting.pro.

name: Electricity for the Entertainment Electrician & Technician
desc: The application of electricity for the theatre, concert stage, television studio, or any other production environment differs from that of a residence or commercial building. Electricity for the Entertainment Electrician & Technician is the only reference on the market that covers these differences, explaining what entertainment electricians and technicians need to know and why. It not only reinforces the fundamentals of electricity, power distribution, and electrical safety, but also explains the latest developments in technology for the live entertainment professional and aspiring professional. Written by an Entertainment Technician Certification Program (ETCP) Recognized trainer, this reference supports practicing electricians and technicians, and provides new technicians the assistance needed for a successful career in the entertainment industry. Fully updated, the second edition contains new information on systems, protocols, regulations, and safety standards, both domestic and international. Additional resources, including conversion tables, voltage spreadsheets, articles from Lighting & Sound International, Lighting & Sound America, and Protocol, and animations and illustrations depicting electricity and electric power distribution developed for the author‘s workshops, can be found on the companion website www.entertainmentelectricity.com.

name: Ugly's Electrical References
desc:
Ugly's Electrical References, 2017 Edition is the on the job reference tool of choice for electrical professionals. Used worldwide by electricians, engineers, contractors, designers, maintenance workers, apprentices, and students Ugly's contains the most commonly required electrical information in an easy to read and easy to access format. 

Updated to reflect the 2017 National Electrical Code (NEC) the new edition features full color diagrams, tables, and illustrations, expanded coverage of alternative energies, and updated electrical safety information. Ugly's offers the most pertinent information used by electricians right at their fingertips, including: mathematical formulas, National Electrical Code tables, wiring configurations, conduit bending, ampacity and conduit fill information, and life saving first aid procedures.

name: Stage Rigging Handbook, Third Edition
desc:
Succinct and jargon free, Stage Rigging Handbook remains the only book in any language that covers the design, operation, and maintenance of stage-rigging equipment. It is written in an at-a-glance outline form, yet contains in-depth information available nowhere else. This fully indexed third edition includes three new parts: the first, an explanation of inspection procedures for rigging systems; the second, a discussion of training in the operation of rigging systems; and the third, essential information about the operation of fire curtains. The remaining six parts, as well as the glossary and bibliography, have been updated. This edition also contains a new preface, many new illustrations, and expanded information on Nicopress terminations.
 
Glerum explains that four main principles make up the core of this book: know the rigging system; keep it in safe working order; know how to use it; and keep your concentration. Glerum applies these principles to all of the major types of stage rigging systems, including block and tackle, hemp, counterweight, and motorized. He describes each type of rigging, then thoroughly reviews the operating procedures and methods of inspecting existing systems.

name: Rigging Math Made Simple, 5th Edition
desc: The fifth edition of Dr. Hall's popular book on rigging math breaks down complex entertainment rigging (theatre and arena) calculations and makes them easy to understand. It also provides hints for remembering many rigging formulas. It is a great resource for anyone studying for either ETCP rigging exam, and includes an explanation of the equations found on the ETCP Certified Rigger - Formula Table. The third edition has a greatly expanded section on arena rigging, as well as more material and appendices for theatrical rigging.

name: The Environmental Guide
desc: Industry reference guide covering environmental and sustainability considerations for live event production and AV installations. Addresses power consumption, equipment disposal, sustainable practices, and green production workflows for the entertainment technology industry. Relevant for AV companies and production professionals looking to reduce their environmental impact.

name: The Book of Transformation
desc: Industry reference covering transformative practices and case studies in AV and live event production. Documents significant productions and technological shifts that have shaped the modern AV and entertainment industry. A forward-looking resource for AV professionals interested in the evolution of production technology and practice.

name: Color and Light: A Guide for the Realist Painter
desc: James Gurney's influential guide to painting realistic light and color — covering color temperature, reflected light, cast shadows, and atmospheric effects. A valuable reference for lighting designers and video content creators who want to understand the physics and perception of light and color in real environments.

name: The Color of Light
desc: Gil Wechsler's guide to theatrical lighting aesthetics and the use of color in stage lighting design. Covers the emotional and visual effects of colored light, color mixing, and how lighting color interacts with costumes, scenery, and performer skin tones in theatrical and live event production.

name: The Light's on Barnum
desc: Lighting designer Shirley Prendergast's account of lighting Barnum on Broadway — offering an insider's perspective on the creative and technical process of designing theatrical lighting for a major Broadway production. A behind-the-scenes look at professional theatrical lighting design in practice.

name: Photometrics Handbook
desc: Photometric data, including beam angles, field angles, foot candles and beam diameters at various throw distances, etc., on over 500 theatrical lighting instruments make this an indispensable reference for lighting designers and electricians.

name: The Practical Guide to Stage Lighting (Second Edition)
desc: Stage Lighting: The Fundamentals is written specifically for introductory stage lighting courses. The book begins with an examination of the nature of light, perception, and color, then leads into a conversation of stage lighting equipment and technicians. Lamps, luminaries, controls/dimming, and electricity form the basis of these chapters. The book also provides a detailed explanation and overview of the lighting design process for the theatre and several other traditional forms of entertainment. Finally, the book explores a variety of additional areas where lighting designers can find related future employment, such as concert and corporate lighting, themed design, architectural and landscape lighting, and computer animation.

name: Stagehand 101 (Second Edition)
desc: This book is a comprehensive manual to help you become a knowledgeable and in demand Stagehand for Live Concerts and Event Production.

name: Practical Cinematography
desc: Practical guide to cinematography covering camera operation, exposure, lenses, lighting for camera, and creative visual storytelling techniques. Applicable to AV professionals who work in video production, broadcast, and live event camera roles. A concise, field-ready reference written for working cinematographers rather than students.

name: The Magic of Light (Second Edition)
desc: The updated second edition of Jean Rosenthal's landmark theatrical lighting design book, revised with additional material and updated equipment references. Covers the fundamentals of theatrical lighting design philosophy, equipment, and creative process from one of Broadway's pioneering lighting designers.

name: Vision and Art
desc: A Harvard neurobiologist explains how vision works, citing the scientific origins of artistic genius and providing coverage of such topics as optical illusions and the correlation between learning disabilities and artistic skill.

name: Theater Technology (Second Edition)
desc:
The most recent innovation brought to theater technology by the eminent theater designer George C. Izenour is a "trans-sondant" curtain, mimicking a wall, which allows halls visually to metamorphose into small or large performance spaces while retaining optimum acoustic quality in both guises. A full set of drawings and photographs of the first trans-sondant structure, in the Vern Riffe Center for the Arts (1995), is one of the new features in the second edition of this incomparable reference guide to theater engineering and technology. 

Izenour ties detailed information on construction, lighting, acoustical structures, electro-mechanical-hydraulic systems, and stage controls to a rich history of technological developments from the invention of the proscenium stage in late Renaissance Italy to the contributions of our own time. All the drawings are produced on the same scale for plan, transverse section, and perspective section. Theater Technology is a reference and source of ideas and inspiration for students, teachers, and practitioners in fields including acoustics, structural engineering, design, lighting, and architecture for the theater. For theater and architecture buffs it offers a detailed and illustrated history of the field and speculation on its future directions.

name: Screen Producing & Media Operations (Second Edition)
desc:
Media servers have established themselves as the dominant video playback tool for live events; however, the practice of delivering content to these systems and the structure of the media operations team is still evolving.

This book outlines a workflow for video content delivery and describes team communication that can be applied to any entertainment production including: television specials, concert touring, corporate events, theater, as well as special events, film, large audience marketing events, and multi-screen permanent installations. This workflow is hardware and software independent, designed to evolve with future technologies as they become established in the field of multi-screen production, and has been proven professionally by the author and her peers over a decade of productions.

The methodology presented will provide insights beneficial to students and current practitioners of media server technology, screens producers, and video content developers. Using real world examples of internationally recognized productions, a foundation is laid for best practices in Media Operations.

Additional content, including full-color versions of the images inside the book, is available online.

name: Stage Rigging Handbook
desc:
Succinct and jargon free, Stage Rigging Handbook remains the only book in any language that covers the design, operation, and maintenance of stage-rigging equipment. It is written in an at-a-glance outline form, yet contains in-depth information available nowhere else. This fully indexed third edition includes three new parts: the first, an explanation of inspection procedures for rigging systems; the second, a discussion of training in the operation of rigging systems; and the third, essential information about the operation of fire curtains. The remaining six parts, as well as the glossary and bibliography, have been updated. This edition also contains a new preface, many new illustrations, and expanded information on Nicopress terminations.
 
Glerum explains that four main principles make up the core of this book: know the rigging system; keep it in safe working order; know how to use it; and keep your concentration. Glerum applies these principles to all of the major types of stage rigging systems, including block and tackle, hemp, counterweight, and motorized. He describes each type of rigging, then thoroughly reviews the operating procedures and methods of inspecting existing systems.

name: Rigging Math Made Simple
desc: The fifth edition of Dr. Hall's popular book on rigging math breaks down complex entertainment rigging (theatre and arena) calculations and makes them easy to understand. It also provides hints for remembering many rigging formulas. It is a great resource for anyone studying for either ETCP rigging exam, and includes an explanation of the equations found on the ETCP Certified Rigger - Formula Table. The third edition has a greatly expanded section on arena rigging, as well as more material and appendices for theatrical rigging.

name: Entertainment Rigging: A Practical Guide for the Entertainment Industry
desc: Harry Donovan's comprehensive guide to entertainment rigging for live events and theatrical production — covering rigging hardware, load calculations, span sets, motors, truss systems, and safety practices. The standard reference for production riggers and fly system operators in the entertainment industry.

name: The Wide Guide: Blueprint for the Multiscreen Movement (Vol. 2)
desc: Reference guide covering multi-screen and widescreen video production techniques, content creation strategies, and technical requirements for panoramic display systems. Addresses the creative and technical challenges of producing content for wide-format and multi-screen event installations. A specialized resource for video designers and content creators working in large-format live event production.

name: Career Projection 101
desc: Professional Projectionist, Clem Harrod, brings over 25 years of knowledge and experience to his company CLEMCO.AV. He established the organization with the mission to Coach, Lead, Educate and Mentor those studying Audio-Visual Technology and working in the Live Event Production Industry. Clem now focuses his energy on helping Independent Contractors enjoy a life with less stress and more financial opportunities. Through his career in Video Projection, Clem began to understand Life Projection in ways that allowed him to amplify his success.

name: Vectorworks for Entertainment Design (Second Edition)
desc: The second edition of the comprehensive guide to using Vectorworks Spotlight for entertainment production design — updated with new software features, improved workflows, and expanded coverage of 3D visualization, rendering, and BIM integration for lighting and production designers.

name: How Video Works: From Broadcast to the Cloud (Second Edition)
desc: The updated second edition of this technical guide covering how video signals are created, processed, distributed, and displayed — expanded to include the latest IP video, UHD, HDR, and cloud delivery technologies. An essential reference for video engineers working across broadcast and live AV production.

name: Technical Theater for Nontechnical People (Second Edition)
desc: In this thoroughly revised new edition, the popular guide firmly embraces the digital age with new content about digital audio, intelligent lighting, LED lighting, video projection, and show control systems, all explained in the same approachable style that has kept this book in the pockets of industry professionals for many years. A brand-new chapter on sound design has also been added, and every chapter has been updated with more information about the basics of theater technology, including draperies, lighting instruments, microphones, costume sketches, and more.

name: The Sound Reinforcement Handbook (Second Edition)
desc: (Yamaha Products). Sound reinforcement is the use of audio amplification systems. This book is the first and only book of its kind to cover all aspects of designing and using such systems for public address and musical performance. The book features information on both the audio theory involved and the practical applications of that theory, explaining everything from microphones to loudspeakers. This revised edition features almost 40 new pages and is even easier to follow with the addition of an index and a simplified page and chapter numbering system. New topics covered include: MIDI, Synchronization, and an Appendix on Logarithms. 416 Pages.

name: The Backstage Handbook
desc: The essential technical reference for theatre, film, and television production professionals — bringing together tools, hardware, materials, electrics, and shop math under one cover. Hundreds of illustrations, tables, and charts covering everything from rigging hardware to lighting equipment.

name: The Ultimate Live Sound Operator's Handbook, Second Edition
desc: The second edition of The Ultimate Live Sound Operator's Handbook is updated with new text, photos, illustrations, and video examples to specifically address the important, cutting-edge concerns faced by modern sound operators of all types. High-quality audio is increasingly imperative, whether you're running sound for a rock, country, punk, or jazz band performing in clubs, arenas, or outdoor parks. With the advent and implementation of large-budget multimedia presentations, along with high-resolution multichannel audio for movies, television, and downloads, any live act must sound great to be well received by today's increasingly savvy audience members. This revised handbook focuses on each aspect of live sound in a way that is straightforward and easy to understand, breaking the process down into principles and practices that assist the modern sound tech in everything from planning and budgeting to mixing and recording the live show.

name: Introduction to Show Control (Second Edition)
desc: The second edition of this comprehensive guide to show control systems — covering MIDI, MSC, OSC, timecode, and network protocols used to integrate and automate audio, video, lighting, and mechanical systems in live event and theatrical production environments.

name: Rock Solid Ethernet (Second Edition)
desc: The second edition of Rock Solid Ethernet — a practical guide to designing and implementing reliable Ethernet networks for live event production. Covers network architecture, managed switches, VLANs, QoS, and troubleshooting strategies for AV-over-IP and show control network environments.

name: Recommended Practice of DMX512 (Second Edition)
desc: The authoritative practical guide to DMX512 protocol for lighting and entertainment control professionals. Covers DMX fundamentals, cable infrastructure, termination, troubleshooting, and best practices drawn directly from the ESTA standard. A must-have reference for lighting technicians working with DMX-controlled systems.

name: Electricity for Entertainment Electricians & Technicians (Second Edition)
desc: Comprehensive practical study guide for entertainment industry power distribution, written by ETCP Recognized Trainer Richard Cadena. Covers AC/DC fundamentals, 3-phase power, grounding, safety, generators, and transformer calculations with real-world examples. The primary resource for anyone preparing for ETCP Entertainment Electrician certification.

name: NFPA 70: National Electrical Code (Current Edition)
desc: The National Electrical Code — the authoritative standard for electrical wiring and safety in the United States. Covers installation requirements, conductor sizing, overcurrent protection, grounding, and special occupancies including assembly venues and stages. Required knowledge for production electricians and AV integrators working on permanent installations.

name: Soares Book on Grounding and Bonding (Second Edition)
desc: The second edition of the authoritative reference for understanding and applying NEC grounding and bonding requirements — updated with current code. Essential reading for production electricians and AV professionals responsible for power distribution and electrical safety.

name: Overcurrents and Undercurrents
desc: A detailed examination of overcurrent protection, electrical safety, and NEC compliance for entertainment industry power systems. Covers breaker selection, feeder sizing, fault current analysis, and the unique challenges of portable power distribution in live event environments. Written specifically for entertainment industry electricians.

name: The MIDI Manual
desc:
The MIDI Manual is a complete reference on MIDI, written by a well-respected sound engineer and author. This best-selling guide provides a clear explanation of what MIDI is, how to use electronic instruments and an explanation of sequencers and how to use them. You will learn how to set up an efficient MIDI system and how to get the best out of your music. 

The MIDI Manual is packed full of useful tips and practical examples on sequencing and mixing techniques. It also covers editors/librarians, working with a score, MIDI in mass media and multimedia and synchronisation. The MIDI spec is set out in detail along with the helpful guidelines on using the implementation chart.

name: Emotional Intelligence by Daniel Goleman
desc: Daniel Goleman's landmark book on emotional intelligence — covering self-awareness, self-regulation, motivation, empathy, and social skills. Essential reading for AV professionals in leadership and client-facing roles who want to develop stronger interpersonal and team communication skills.

name: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People
desc: Stephen Covey's timeless guide to personal and professional effectiveness covering seven foundational habits including being proactive, beginning with the end in mind, and seeking first to understand before being understood. Widely considered essential reading for anyone in a leadership or team role.

name: Leadership and Self-Deception
desc: The Arbinger Institute's business fable about how self-deception undermines leadership and team performance. Through a compelling narrative, it reveals how working from a mindset of 'being in the box' sabotages relationships and results — and how to shift to an outward-focused, collaborative approach.

name: How to Win Friends and Influence People
desc: Dale Carnegie's classic guide to human relations covering how to make friends, influence others, handle disagreements, and win people to your way of thinking without conflict. One of the best-selling self-help books in history and essential reading for anyone working in client-facing production roles.

name: Good to Great
desc: Jim Collins' research-based study of what transforms good companies into great ones — identifying key principles including Level 5 Leadership, the Hedgehog Concept, and a culture of discipline. Highly relevant for AV business owners and production managers building high-performing organizations.

name: Drive: The Surprising Truth About What Motivates Us
desc: Daniel Pink's exploration of the science of human motivation — revealing that autonomy, mastery, and purpose are the true drivers of performance, not external rewards. Valuable reading for AV professionals and managers who want to build motivated, self-directed teams.

name: Atomic Habits
desc: James Clear's practical framework for building good habits and breaking bad ones through small, incremental improvements. Covers the four laws of behavior change and how to design your environment for success. One of the most actionable books on personal productivity available.

name: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team
desc: Patrick Lencioni's business fable identifying the five core dysfunctions that undermine team performance: absence of trust, fear of conflict, lack of commitment, avoidance of accountability, and inattention to results. Essential reading for production managers and team leaders in AV.

name: Light Bytes by Wayne Howell
desc: Practical reference guide covering DMX512, RDM, and show control protocols for lighting technicians and system designers. Written by the author of the DMX512 standard with deep technical insight into protocol behavior, troubleshooting, and system integration. A go-to resource for anyone working with intelligent lighting control infrastructure.

name: DMX512 for the Moving Light Programmer
desc: A practical guide to DMX512 protocol for moving light programmers — covering DMX addressing, channel mapping, fixture profiles, and the technical aspects of controlling intelligent lighting fixtures via DMX. A useful reference for lighting technicians and programmers working with moving lights.

name: The AV Handbook
desc: A comprehensive reference guide covering all aspects of audio-visual technology — including signal flow, display systems, projection, audio systems, control, and AV system integration. A broad technical reference for AV professionals working across installed and live event production environments.

name: Humble Inquiry
desc: Edgar Schein's guide to asking good questions rather than giving answers or directives — covering how a humble, curious approach to communication builds better relationships, surfaces important information, and creates more effective teams. Essential reading for AV project managers and team leads.

name: The Fearless Organization
desc: Amy Edmondson's research-based guide to creating psychological safety in teams — where people feel safe to speak up, take risks, and admit mistakes without fear of punishment. Highly relevant for production companies and AV teams building a culture of open communication and continuous improvement.

name: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team (Patrick Lencioni)
desc: Patrick Lencioni's business fable about the five dysfunctions that destroy team cohesion and performance. Through a compelling narrative, it provides a practical model for building trust, managing conflict constructively, and holding each other accountable in high-pressure production environments.

name: The Backstage Handbook (4th Edition)
desc: The fourth edition of the essential technical reference for theatre, film, and television production — updated with current equipment, materials, and standards. Brings together tools, hardware, rigging, electrics, and shop math in one comprehensive illustrated reference.

name: Control Freak by Wayne Howell
desc: Wayne Howell's guide to show control and automation for live entertainment — covering control protocols, system design, and the practical implementation of automated show systems. Written by the founder of Artistic Licence, a leading manufacturer of lighting control products.

name: The AV Handbook Vol. 2
desc: Volume 2 of the comprehensive AV Handbook series — covering additional topics in audio-visual technology including advanced signal distribution, display calibration, system integration, and emerging AV technologies for professionals working in installed and live event AV.

name: CompTIA Network+ Study Guide
desc: The CompTIA Network+ Study Guide covers all exam objectives for the Network+ certification — including IP networking, subnetting, network protocols, security, and troubleshooting. A foundational networking certification relevant for AV professionals working with AV-over-IP and enterprise AV installations.

name: Photometrics Handbook (3rd Edition)
desc: The third edition of the Photometrics Handbook providing updated standardized photometric data for contemporary stage lighting instruments — including LED, laser, and modern discharge fixtures alongside traditional incandescent sources. The essential data reference for lighting designers and specifiers.

name: AV Coloring Book by AV Educate
desc: AV Educate's own coloring book designed to make learning AV concepts engaging and accessible. Features illustrations of AV equipment, signal flow diagrams, and production scenarios — a fun and unique educational resource for students and newcomers entering the AV industry.

name: How to Be an Engineer
desc: An accessible introduction to engineering concepts and the engineering mindset — covering how engineers approach problem solving, design thinking, and technical challenges. A useful resource for AV professionals who want to develop a more systematic, engineering-focused approach to system design.

name: The AudioVisual Adventure (Children's)
desc: An educational children's book introducing young readers to the exciting world of audio-visual technology and live event production. Designed to inspire curiosity about AV careers and technology in children and families connected to the entertainment industry.

name: My First Book of Electricity and Electronics
desc: An illustrated introduction to electricity and electronics concepts for young readers — covering circuits, batteries, conductors, and basic electronic components. A fun way to introduce children to the foundational concepts that underpin professional audio and AV technology.

name: Rosie Revere, Engineer
desc: Andrea Beaty's beloved picture book about a girl who dreams of being an engineer — emphasizing creativity, perseverance, and the importance of learning from failure. A popular recommendation for introducing young people to STEM careers and the engineering mindset.

name: Filmset Chronicles by Roberta Sneve
desc: Roberta Sneve's account of life on a film set — offering an insider's perspective on the people, culture, and practical realities of working in film and television production. A personal and entertaining look at the behind-the-scenes world of professional film production.

name: Live Sound Reinforcement
desc: Excellent book to introduce beginners and house-of-worship volunteers to live sound reinforcement. Readers will learn about sound as experts share their experiences, along with tips and tricks for working in the field.

name: Mastering Audio: The Art and the Science
desc: Written by Grammy award-winning mastering engineer Bob Katz, Mastering Audio gives a thorough introduction to the unique procedures and technical issues involved in mastering — from monitoring and dithering to loudness, metering, and multi-channel audio. The industry standard reference on mastering.

name: Acoustics and Psychoacoustics
desc: Gives an essential grounding in how real music sounds behave in different spaces whether during a performance or a recording. Covers the physics of sound, human hearing, and the relationship between acoustics and perceived audio quality.

name: Tour:Smart: And Break the Band
desc: From packing the right equipment to keeping enough gas in the tank to get home, every aspect of making a successful tour with a band is addressed in this comprehensive guide for touring production professionals.

name: The Worst Gig
desc: Everybody from Rush and The Sex Pistols to Owl City and Fitz & the Tantrums can be found in helpfully titled sections like Wrong Venue, Insane Fans, Dangerous Malfunctions, Nature's Wrath, Violence and Oops. A collection of the industry's most memorable disaster stories.

name: Kim Gordon: Girl in a Band
desc: Kim Gordon, founding member of Sonic Youth, tells her story — a memoir of life as an artist, of music, marriage, motherhood, and independence, written as one of the first women of rock and roll. A candid look at the inner workings of the downtown New York music scene.

name: Alice Bag: Violence Girl: East L.A. Rage to Hollywood Stage
desc: Born to Mexican-born and American-naturalized parents, Alicia Armendariz migrated to participate in the birth of the 1970s punk movement as lead singer for The Bags. A Chicana punk story covering the early LA punk scene and the cultural divide between East LA and Hollywood.

name: Viv Albertine: Clothes, Clothes, Clothes. Music, Music, Music. Boys, Boys, Boys.
desc: The guitarist for seminal female punk group The Slits recounts playing with Sid Vicious, touring with the Clash, dating Mick Jones, and inspiring Train in Vain. A raw and honest memoir from one of punk's most important female instrumentalists.

name: Patti Smith: Just Kids
desc: Patti Smith's memoir offering a never-before-seen glimpse of her remarkable relationship with photographer Robert Mapplethorpe in the epochal days of New York City and the Chelsea Hotel in the late sixties and seventies. An honest and moving story of youth, friendship, and artistic development.

name: Girls to the Front: The True Story of the Riot Grrrl Revolution
desc: The epic, definitive history of Riot Grrrl — the radical feminist uprising that exploded into the public eye in the 1990s including incendiary punk bands Bikini Kill, Bratmobile, Heavens to Betsy, and Huggy Bear. A dynamic chronicle of a movement and an era.

name: Cinderella's Big Score: Women of the Punk and Indie Underground
desc: Celebrates the contributions of punk's oft-overlooked female artists and explores the latent sexism of indie rock. Profiles groups from The Runaways and The Slits to L7, Sleater-Kinney, and Le Tigre, with rare interviews and photos illuminating how seminal women of the 70s and 80s shaped female rockers of today.

name: The Lost Women of Rock Music: Female Musicians of the Punk Era
desc: In the late 1970s and early 1980s, female guitarists, bass-players, keyboardists and drummers began playing in rock bands for the first time. This book investigates the social and commercial reasons why these women became lost from the rock music record, and rewrites this period of popular music history.

name: Musicophilia: Tales of Music and the Brain
desc: Oliver Sacks explores the place music occupies in the brain and how it affects the human condition — covering musical hallucinations, synesthesia, absolute pitch, and the uncanny power of music to move people with neurological conditions. A fascinating intersection of neuroscience and music.

name: This Is Your Brain On Music: The Science of a Human Obsession
desc: Daniel Levitin uses cutting-edge science to answer why music makes us feel the way it does. Covering everything from why we enjoy sad songs to the neuroscience of rhythm and melody, this accessible exploration of music and the brain is essential reading for anyone passionate about why music matters.

name: The Rest Is Noise: Listening to the Twentieth Century
desc: A voyage into the labyrinth of modern music — from Strauss and Mahler through jazz, atonality, minimalism, and beyond. Alex Ross illuminates a century of musical revolution through compelling narrative, making the obscure world of 20th century classical composition accessible and fascinating.

name: Sound Unseen: Acousmatic Sound in Theory and Practice
desc: The first major study in English of Pierre Schaeffer's theory of acousmatics — the art of sounds heard without seeing their source. An essential text for scholars of philosophy of music, electronic music, sound studies, and the history of the senses.

name: The Sound Book: The Science of the Sonic Wonders of the World
desc: Acoustic engineer Trevor Cox hunts for the sonic wonders of the world — creaking glaciers, whispering galleries, stalactite organs, musical roads, and a Mayan pyramid that chirps like a bird. With forays into archaeology, neuroscience, and design, Cox explains how sound is made and altered by environment.

name: Sound Systems: Design and Optimization
desc: Bob McCarthy's definitive technical guide to modern sound system design and optimization — thorough, complete, methodical, and passionate about the laws and facts of physics. Includes rules-of-thumb and cheat-sheets for instant insight into complex system behavior. The industry's essential reference for audio system designers and engineers.
